gaps.title: Unified Context Budget Manager with Real-Time Token Allocation
gaps.description: No tool provides real-time, policy-driven token budget allocation across context window zones (system prompt, memory, RAG results, tool outputs, conversation history). Teams manually manage this zero-sum game. Need: a middleware that dynamically allocates token budgets per zone, tracks consumption step-by-step, and auto-triggers compression/eviction before overflow.
gaps.evidence: Context allocation is a zero-sum game without explicit allocation policies. Tool outputs can balloon context, and agents fail with 'prompt is too long' errors. 0/18 tools in the matrix provide dedicated prompt caching or token budget management.
gaps.affected_tools: All orchestration frameworks lack this as a first-class feature

gaps.title: Automated Prompt Optimization for Agent Systems (beyond DSPy)
gaps.description: Only DSPy and Braintrust offer automated prompt optimization, but DSPy lacks agent memory/multi-agent support, and Braintrust lacks context management. No tool combines automated prompt tuning WITH agent context awareness -- optimizing prompts while considering what's in the context window, tool outputs, and memory state.
gaps.evidence: Only 2/18 tools (11%) support automated prompt optimization. DSPy optimizes prompts in isolation; agent frameworks rely on manual prompt engineering. The gap is an agent-aware prompt optimizer.
gaps.affected_tools: DSPy (no agent awareness), Braintrust (no context management)

gaps.title: Context Compression Middleware for Production Agent Pipelines
gaps.description: While context compression is discussed widely, no standalone tool provides plug-and-play compression middleware that sits between agent frameworks and LLM APIs. Teams need: automatic summarization of tool outputs, semantic deduplication, priority-aware compression, and cost tracking -- as a drop-in library, not a full framework replacement.
gaps.evidence: Context compression can reduce tokens 50-80% while preserving accuracy. LangGraph mentions it but lacks dedicated tooling. The context-engineering-toolkit on GitHub is early-stage. Production teams are building custom solutions.
gaps.affected_tools: Context Engineering Toolkit (early/limited), LangGraph (basic), most tools lack this entirely

gaps.title: Cross-Framework Agent Memory Interoperability Layer
gaps.description: Memory systems (Mem0, Zep, Letta) are siloed and don't interoperate with prompt management tools (PromptLayer, Vellum) or orchestration frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI) in a standardized way. No 'MCP for memory' standard exists. Teams building with CrewAI + Mem0 + PromptLayer must write custom glue code.
gaps.evidence: The memory space is compared to 'vector databases in 2022' -- fragmented with consolidation approaching. MCP covers tool integration but not memory/context standards. No memory tool supports prompt versioning; no prompt tool supports persistent memory.
gaps.affected_tools: All memory and prompt tools operate in silos

gaps.title: Context Quality Scoring and Observability Dashboard
gaps.description: No tool provides context-specific observability: measuring context precision (signal-to-noise), context freshness (stale data detection), context conflicts (contradictory sources), and context utilization (which parts the model actually attends to). Existing observability tools track latency/cost but not context quality.
gaps.evidence: Context failures -- missing, stale, conflicting, or irrelevant -- cause most enterprise AI breakdowns. Models show 'lost in the middle' degradation. Attention is non-uniform across long contexts. No observability tool measures context quality specifically.
gaps.affected_tools: LangSmith, Langfuse, Braintrust (track cost/latency, not context quality)

gaps.title: Enterprise Context Engineering Platform (beyond artisanal per-app)
gaps.description: Current context engineering is 'artisanal and bespoke' per application. Enterprises running dozens of agents need: shared context policies, cross-agent context governance, centralized memory management, consistent context quality standards, and compliance-aware context handling. No platform serves this enterprise orchestration layer.
gaps.evidence: Context engineering doesn't scale across organizations -- it ends up being artisanal. Well-crafted prompts can't solve systemic challenges of running agents across an enterprise. DataHub calls context management 'the missing piece for agentic AI'.
gaps.affected_tools: No tool addresses enterprise-wide context governance

gaps.title: Visual Context Window Debugger for Agent Development
gaps.description: Developers building agents cannot visualize what's in their context window at each step: how much space is used by system prompt vs memory vs RAG vs tool outputs vs conversation. No tool provides a visual debugger showing context composition, token allocation, and attention patterns in real-time during agent development.
gaps.evidence: Developers debug context issues blindly. Tool outputs can balloon context unexpectedly. No visual tool shows context window composition in real-time. JetBrains research highlights the need for smarter context management visibility.
gaps.affected_tools: No existing tool provides this capability

gaps.title: Intelligent Context Prefetch and Predictive Loading for Agents
gaps.description: Agents reactively fetch context (RAG retrieval after user query). No tool provides predictive context loading: anticipating what context an agent will need based on conversation trajectory, task type, and historical patterns, then pre-loading it to reduce latency.
gaps.evidence: Context retrieval adds latency to every agent step. Prompt caching helps for static prefixes but not dynamic context. No tool (0/18) addresses predictive context loading. This mirrors CPU cache prefetching patterns.
gaps.affected_tools: All RAG tools are reactive, not predictive

gaps.title: Prompt Template Marketplace with Context-Aware Composition
gaps.description: Prompt management tools (PromptHub, PromptLayer) treat prompts as isolated templates. No marketplace lets teams share, compose, and version prompt COMPONENTS (not just full prompts) that are context-aware: adapting based on available memory, RAG results, and user state.
gaps.evidence: PromptHub has Git-style versioning for full prompts. No tool supports modular prompt components that compose based on runtime context. Teams copy-paste and adapt prompts across projects without reusable building blocks.

gaps.title: Multi-Modal Context Orchestration (text + code + images + structured data)
gaps.description: Most context engineering tools handle text context only. As agents process images, code, structured data, and audio, no tool provides unified multi-modal context management: prioritizing, compressing, and orchestrating different modality types within the same context window.
gaps.evidence: Graphlit offers multimodal semantic memory but lacks context window management. LLMs increasingly support multi-modal inputs but tooling hasn't caught up. Agents processing code + text + images need modality-aware context allocation.
gaps.affected_tools: Graphlit (memory only, no orchestration), all others are text-focused

gaps.title: Context-Aware Cost Optimization Engine
gaps.description: LLM costs scale linearly with context length, but no tool optimizes cost vs quality tradeoffs in real-time: routing simpler sub-tasks to smaller/cheaper models, compressing context for cost-sensitive steps, and caching aggressively for repeated patterns. Current tools track cost but don't actively optimize it.
gaps.evidence: Long inputs are expensive even with prefix caching. Attention complexity scales quadratically. No tool dynamically optimizes the cost-quality tradeoff for context. Teams overpay by stuffing full context into every call.
gaps.affected_tools: Observability tools report cost but don't optimize it

gaps.title: Temporal Context Decay and Freshness Management
gaps.description: Only Zep tracks how facts change over time. No tool provides temporal decay policies: automatically deprioritizing or evicting stale context, flagging contradictions between old and new information, and ensuring agents always work with the freshest relevant context.
gaps.evidence: Only 1/18 tools (6%) supports temporal memory. Enterprises find AI using stale data from old reports mixed with current data. Context freshness is a top pain point but almost entirely unaddressed.
gaps.affected_tools: Zep (partial), all others ignore temporal aspects

gaps.title: Agent Context Replay and Testing Framework
gaps.description: No tool lets developers replay agent sessions with different context configurations to debug and optimize. Teams need: capture context snapshots at each step, replay with modified context, A/B test context strategies (e.g., more memory vs more RAG), and measure impact on task completion.
gaps.evidence: Braintrust offers prompt A/B testing but not context configuration testing. Evaluation tools test prompts in isolation, not context strategies. Debugging context issues requires reproducing entire agent sessions.
gaps.affected_tools: Braintrust (prompt-level only), no tool supports context-level replay

gaps.title: Lightweight Context Engineering SDK for Small Teams
gaps.description: Current solutions require either full framework adoption (LangChain/LangGraph) or expensive SaaS platforms (Vellum, Humanloop). Small teams and solo developers need a lightweight, open-source SDK that provides context window management, basic memory, and prompt versioning without the overhead of a full framework.
gaps.evidence: LangChain has a steep learning curve with heavy abstractions. SaaS tools start at $25-49/mo but lock in. The context-engineering-toolkit on GitHub shows demand but is early-stage. PydanticAI and Mirascope are lightweight but lack context management.
gaps.affected_tools: Context Engineering Toolkit (too early), PydanticAI/Mirascope (no context management)

gaps.title: MCP-Native Context Router for Multi-Tool Agent Workflows
gaps.description: MCP standardizes tool connections (10K+ servers) but provides no intelligence about WHICH tools/context to fetch and WHEN. Need: an MCP-native router that dynamically selects which MCP servers to query based on the current task, user intent, and available context budget, rather than the agent making all decisions.
gaps.evidence: MCP has 10K+ servers and 75+ connectors but no routing intelligence. Anthropic's Tool Search helps but is API-specific. Agent frameworks implement their own tool selection logic without MCP-aware context routing.
gaps.affected_tools: MCP (protocol only, no routing), LangChain/CrewAI (custom tool selection)
